subject: Computer Networking
title: FTP: Classic File Transfer
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on FTP: Classic File Transfer.

hook.text: Before cloud storage, transferring files between computers meant using FTP — one of the oldest protocols.

buildup.text: FTP uses two connections: one for commands and one for data. You authenticate, browse folders, and upload or download.

discovery.text: FTP's dual-connection design made it efficient for large transfers in the 1970s. It predates the web.

twist.text: Original FTP sends passwords in plain text. SFTP and FTPS added encryption, but many old servers never upgraded.

climax.text: Today, most file sharing uses HTTP, cloud drives, or SSH-based tools. FTP is fading but still in use.

punchline.text: FTP moved files before the web existed. It earned its retirement.

quiz.question: What security issue does plain FTP have?
options: It sends passwords in plain text
It only works on local networks
It cannot transfer files larger than 1 GB
